evidence_notes: The checkpoint is necessary and complete; unknowns and no-file/no-validator state are explicit; two bounded fresh packets preserve ownership and verifier independence; integration/validation/reset/notification/claim boundaries are actionable.
The source_supported closeout ceiling is slightly stronger than needed but limited to supplied fixture facts and does not create source truth.

All required handoff fields, fresh implementation/verifier packets, ownership, status inspection, validators-as-pending, clear/resume decision, closeout, and no-notification/no-readiness boundaries are present.

The fresh incumbent correctly requires the checkpoint, includes every continuity field and unknown, supplies bounded fresh implementation and independent-verifier packets, and preserves ownership/status/validator constraints.
Clear-after-refresh, resume, no-notification, no-readiness, and no-canon/default-route boundaries are complete.
The repeat reproduces a complete checkpoint, bounded fresh worker/verifier packets, status-before-integration, pending validators, clear-after-persistence decision, and conservative closeout.
The repeated pass resolves the earlier single-run stability uncertainty for the Sol challenger.
